free_text: Independent cross sections of Na, K, Rb, Cs and Fr
isotopes produced in Ta and U targets bombarded by
C-12 ions up to 77 MeV/nucleon.
It is O1545
Details of experiment.
Last checking has been done.
.The results are compared to those obtained by
nuclear reactions induced by protons over a wide energy
range.
*By authors*.The absolute cross section are obtained by
radiochemical determination of Cs-132 due to the
measurements of Lund et.al.(Private communication) with
an experimental error estimated at 10%.This uncertainty
is included in the cross section results.
*By authors*.The sodium isotopic distribution is
corrected for the contribution due to a reaction with
the carbon catcher foils using a blank experiment
without target material deposited on the foils.
The K-39 and K-41 are not measurable since the natural
background is overwhelming.
The target-oven assembly includes a thin wall(0.5 mm)
cylindric graphite oven and stack of thin graphite
foils(5-mg/cm**2).
A layer(1-mg/cm**2) of target material is deposited
on the surface of every third foil. These target foils
are separated from each other by two graphite foils
used as catchers. The assembly consists of 30 target
foils and 60 catchers.
The uranium target was made of pure U-238 prepared by
electromagnetic isotope separation.
The natural tantalum target can be considered as
monoisotopic(Ta-180 approx.equal 10E-03*Ta-181)
it was prepared by direct evaporation of bulk metal
onto the graphite foils.
The total thickness of U and Ta is 30-mg/cm**2,
graphite is 450-mg/cm**2.
A three corrections have been done:
-an isotopic discrimination,
-beta decay of isotopes of interest during the
diffusion process,
-the cumulative effect due to beta decay of several
generations of parents inside the target.
.The error is reported by authors in table.
